$description: Phrases the doctrine forbids in customer-facing or doctrine-internal copy. Sources: Part 1 §16 (anti-patterns) and Part 14 §2.3 (banned phrases). Any addition requires a Doctrine RFC per 00-audit-and-roadmap.md §12.2.

block.reason: Confidence-claiming without justification. Show, don't tell.
block.alternative: Describe what specifically the system does, with measurable evidence.

block.reason: Implies the user's effort doesn't count; minimises friction we should acknowledge.
block.alternative: Be specific about what's automated vs manual.
Name the benchmark and the year of comparison.
Reference a specific class and the metric it beats on.
Describe the specific change vs the prior state of the art.
Name the specific composition that creates value.
Describe the specific outcome that pleases the user.
Be specific about which class and the metric.

block.reason: Non-descriptive link text — fails WCAG 2.4.4 Link Purpose.
block.alternative: Use the destination's name as the link text.

block.reason: Manufactured urgency on non-promotional surfaces — Part 5 §21 trauma-informed forbids.
block.alternative: Drop on trauma-informed surfaces; on promotional surfaces, state the actual end date.
State what happened and what to do next.

block.reason: Trivialises the user's experience of the failure.
block.alternative: Describe what went wrong; do not anthropomorphise the system.

block.reason: Useless — names the category, not the problem.
block.alternative: Describe what failed, why, and what the user can do next.

warn.reason: Cliche; back with a specific metric (e.g., LCP ≤ 1.8s p75).
Imprecise; describe what specifically is robust.
Claim about user perception; only validated by user testing.
